FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Regional bank M&A accelerates with First Hawaiian's $2B TriCo buy, while Q2 earnings set up strong on SpaceX/volatility but rate-hike risks loom.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian acquiring TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks Hawaiian's return to mainland U.S. after BNP Paribas exit.
Fed rate-hike odds for July climbing on Strait of Hormuz oil-price spike; direct NIM compression and deposit-pricing headwind for the sector.
Big banks positioned for strong Q2 earnings on SpaceX IPO advisory fees, geopolitical volatility trading, and commercial lending pickup.
JPMorgan names Doug Petno and Troy Rohrbaugh as co-presidents; Dimon says Mary Erdoes chose retirement—characterizes as 'natural change.'

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Major wealth management consolidation continues with Russell's CalPERS acquisition and RIA roll-up acceleration, while litigation clouds emerge around digital advisory cash practices.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments for $580B pension system; deal lets Russell shed debt burden and adopt non-profit governance model similar to Vanguard's reputational playbook.
RIA consolidation hits overdrive: Hightower sees record organic growth, Choreo closes fourth acquisition post-SpaceX exit, and ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform as sector reshuffles.
Carson Group restructures around W-2 and independent RIA channels with separate home office teams to sharpen focus and boost organic growth amid industry roll-up frenzy.
Betterment hit with class action lawsuit over cash sweep accounts—allegations that firm directed client cash into non-interest-bearing deposits while capturing spread profits.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: U.S. P&C industry hits decade-high profitability amid pricing discipline, while geopolitical risk in the Hormuz Strait and regional consolidation reshape underwriting dynamics.

bullets.text: U.S. P&C combined ratio hit 10-year low in 2025 with $84B underwriting profit over two years, driven by disciplined pricing and underwriting—but divergence across lines signals winners and losers.
Hormuz Strait tanker attacks escalating geopolitical risk in a corridor handling 21% of global crude, likely spiking war-risk marine insurance premiums and creating P&L volatility for underwriters.
Saudi Re deepens Lloyd's play with 22.5% stake in AdA Risk (£8.95M), signaling regional appetite for established London market infrastructure.
RockRose Risk launches AI-driven homeowners product in California tying mitigation incentives to underwriting, targeting market segment abandoned by traditional carriers.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: 5 stories spanning payments assets, credit divergence, and regulatory headwinds—Mastercard weighs Vocalink exit while CMBS stress signals servicer activity ahead.

bullets.text: Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink, the UK payments platform acquired from British banks in 2016—potential portfolio optimization or forced exit.
CMBS special servicing rate climbed in June 2026 driven by large retail, office, and lodging distress—watch for servicer workout activity and credit deterioration.
North America private debt still LP-preferred despite noise; AlbaCore flags widening gap between performing and distressed credits in the market.
Klarna hit with Dutch class action lawsuit; Nubank cleared for full Mexican banking ops—fintech regulatory/litigation risks vs. expansion upside.

PE & Deals.lede: PE financing and secondaries heating up: Arctos debuts novel GP financing structure, Moelis expands secondaries platform, and mega-LPs pile into energy infrastructure.

bullets.text: Arctos closes $6.2B GP financing using 'self-liquidating' Keystone structure—first of its kind in GP financing market, enabling capital returns to LPs while maintaining portfolio exposure.
Moelis hires Evercore VP to build LP-led secondaries practice with four-person team transfer, capitalizing on expanding market as LPs seek liquidity at favorable valuations.
Blackstone, Apollo, KKR co-invest $5.34B in Williams' energy infrastructure projects, signaling institutional conviction on large-cap PE energy plays amid favorable market conditions.

Markets & Macro.lede: Bank earnings season opens with high stakes; QT risks loom as QE reshaped bank funding; small caps rally on tech rotation; ECB advances digital euro pilots; UK gilts spike on fiscal concerns.

bullets.text: Bank earnings kick off Tuesday with market braced for disappointment after running high expectations—early signal on whether lending margins can hold up.
QE structurally rewired bank balance sheets to depend on central bank liquidity; quantitative tightening now poses underestimated risk to stability, argues FT's Cetina.
Russell 2000 on track for best year since 2003 as capital flows rotate from mega-cap tech into small caps on valuation relief and AI capex cycle shifts.
UK 10-year gilt yields breach 5% on oil surge and fiscal concerns just as new PM takes office—market signaling anxiety about debt sustainability.

Newsletters.lede: Big bank earnings drive macro sentiment; tech disruption via Chinese AI; structural shifts in education finance and advisor workflows.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—critical read on credit quality, deposit dynamics, and consumer health across lending and trading.
Alibaba's cheaper AI model ranks first globally, signaling potential erosion of US tech dominance in a critical sector.
Student loan policy shifts are forcing advisors to rebuild college planning strategies, reshaping demand for education financing products and advisory services.
Vanguard-Envestnet partnership tackles tax friction in model portfolio conversions—a workflow optimization that could reshape RIA economics.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: 4 stories spanning incentive misalignment in sports, computational theory's role in market competition, startup capital creativity in AI, and Apple's strategic AI weakness.

bullets.text: NBA players gaming individual stats at team expense reveals classic principal-agent failure—performance metrics reward selfish behavior over winning.
If P = NP, firms can efficiently detect and sustain cartels; competitive markets depend on P ≠ NP making collusion detection computationally intractable.
Groq's Jonathan Ross secured $20B NVIDIA deal via unconventional financing (Grok Bonds) while near-bankrupt—a masterclass in startup capital creativity under pressure.
Apple's OpenAI lawsuit over employee trade-secret theft masks the real issue: Apple has no coherent AI strategy and is strategically behind.

all.lede: Regional bank M&A heats up as First Hawaiian buys TriCo for $2B, while major bank earnings kick off tomorrow—and QT risks loom larger than Fed realizes.

bullets.text: First Hawaiian acquires TriCo Bancshares for $2B, closing by year-end—marks Hawaiian's return to mainland US after BNP Paribas exit.
Big Six US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow; results will signal credit quality, deposit health, and consumer resilience across lending and wealth management.
Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink (UK payments platform acquired 2016) back to original owners, signaling potential strategic shift in payments M&A.
QE structurally rewired bank balance sheets and funding models, making quantitative tightening riskier than policymakers realize due to persistent central bank liquidity dependence.
